flags.title: Meta — New Sales Ad auto-killed · $439.87 spend · ROAS 0.73
flags.problem: New Sales Ad (ID 120248769281250118) crossed the $75 kill threshold at ROAS 0.726. 90% of budget went to women 65+ at ROAS 0.96. Ad auto-paused by A-1 authority.
flags.deadline: Today — replacement needed before daily budget resets

solutions.recommendation: Launch a male-targeted replacement with hard age lock 25–54 and Advantage+ OFF. Male segment showed ROAS 2.34 at only 9% of budget — isolate and scale that signal.
solutions.action: In Meta Ads Manager: duplicate the paused ad set → set age 25–54 → disable Advantage+ Audience → set budget $25/day → activate. Post confirmation to #stg-ads-watchdog.

solutions.recommendation: Before relaunching, test the same video with a female 35–54 segment separately. The 65+ age skew may be a targeting artifact, not a creative problem — isolating by age will confirm.
solutions.action: Create a second ad set: female only, age 35–54, Advantage+ OFF, $15/day. Run both male and female variants in parallel for 5 days before deciding which to scale.

flags.title: Amazon Bundle B0GT1H84JM — $169.37 spend · $0 sales
flags.problem: Save the Shave Bundle has $169.37 Amazon ad spend with zero attributed sales. Real waste gate triggered (spend ≥ $75, zero return). No Blaze write authority on Amazon.
flags.deadline: Today — every hour adds ~$7 in wasted spend

solutions.recommendation: Pause the Amazon campaign for this ASIN immediately. Bundle ads often suffer from keyword mismatch — searchers looking for individual products land on a bundle page and bounce.
solutions.action: Amazon Ads → Campaign Manager → Save the Shave Bundle campaign → pause. Pull search term report and identify irrelevant queries before reactivating.

solutions.recommendation: After pausing, switch the bundle campaign to exact-match keywords only targeting 'dematting kit' and 'dog mat removal kit' — broad match is likely the waste driver.
solutions.action: In the paused campaign: remove all broad/phrase match targets → add 5–10 exact match keywords → set bids at $0.75 → reactivate at $10/day test budget.

flags.title: Checking balance $13,283 — below $25K watch threshold
flags.problem: STG checking account at $13,283 — below the $25K liquid cash floor. AP $48,504 is 100% overdue. Combined cash is $587K but daily operating cash is critically low.

solutions.recommendation: Transfer $30–40K from money market to checking immediately to restore the operating buffer. Then contact all overdue AP vendors with a payment schedule — 100% past due is a vendor relationship risk.
solutions.action: Patrick: log into QB → transfer $35K from money market to checking → send payment schedule to all AP vendors with dates → confirm with Will by 3 PM today.

flags.title: Reviews — 3.8 avg · 4 negative · sheet 12 days stale
flags.problem: Review sheet hasn't been updated in 12 days. Latest batch averages 3.8 stars with 4 one/two-star reviews. Complaint themes going unmonitored.

solutions.recommendation: Angelo updates the review sheet this week. Once updated, pull the 4 negative reviews and identify complaint themes — mat removal difficulty and brush quality are the most common patterns per prior learnings.
solutions.action: Will: follow up with Angelo to update the STG Weekly Review Update sheet. Review the 4 negative entries for product or listing issues that can be addressed in copy or images.

flags.title: Klaviyo — Browse Abandonment still in DRAFT · all flows zero-send
flags.problem: Browse Abandonment flow has been in DRAFT — not live. All 7-day flows show zero sends. Suppressed email revenue that should be capturing browse signals daily.

solutions.recommendation: Marcelo activates the Browse Abandonment flow in Klaviyo. This is a high-intent segment — each day it stays in DRAFT is missed revenue from warm visitors who showed product interest.
solutions.action: Marcelo: Klaviyo → Flows → Browse Abandonment → set to Live. Confirm in #stg-morning-brief when done.
